author: Q | Taitus | LMRUstur
content:
According to the chart shown, over the past four weeks, ATMTA has generated total revenue of 1,013,626 USDC, of which approximately 1.1% (11,027 USDC) comes from market fees collected in USDC. When analyzing the composition of this revenue, it becomes clear that the main source has been the sale of the ATLAS token, which accounts for around 646,000 dollars, or 64% of the total.

On the other hand, purchasing a ship in the market has become increasingly difficult due to the presence of bots, which operate with large amounts of USDC and automated systems. These bots gain an advantage over regular users by snapping up the best deals and consistently being the first in buy orders. While this dynamic cannot be completely eliminated, there is a potential solution to help balance the environment.

If ship transactions in the secondary market were limited exclusively to ATLAS, ATMTA would give up USDC fees, but would still collect revenue in ATLAS. This change would bring multiple benefits: bots would be forced to convert large amounts of USDC into ATLAS in order to place sell orders, creating more volume and demand for the token. In addition, they would need to hold ATLAS in their wallets to maintain flexibility and adjust their orders, contributing to greater circulation and utility of the ATLAS token within the ecosystem. In this scenario, all users would benefit.

author: [SH]b0b [STAR]
content:
Hey, team! I actively play UE5 and want to leave my feedback.
The game is fascinating, I really want to log in and play every day. The game is skill-dependent and the progression in leveling up characters became the catalyst that was missing. In addition to what @Deleted User is already working on, here are a few things that can make game even better:
Ping in the lobby for each server;,Points of interest on the map, personally I would add some kind of boosts, like Double Damage / Double Boost (increased/unlimited stamina bar), appearing on a timer, or randomly with a notification;,Additional experience for killing players with streaks. Players model with 5/10 series of kills starts glowing, making them more desirable victim;,As for experience, I would like that in modes like Team Deathmatch or Surge, where you are forced to play as a certain character, this character was just a skin, and the experience went to the crew member you logged in with (I logged in as an epic punaab in Surge, I play as Ustur 1body, but the experience goes to punaab with 10% bonus from rarity). Players who want to level up a certain character, or just losers with family/work/friends who do not have so much time to play games will be happy with this adjustment.,In my opinion, grenades need some improvement. They seem like toys, unless you throw them at punaabs, I would also like to see a clearer stun effect and a larger explosion radius, ideally the damage should be uneven and increase towards the center of the explosion.,Double XP Weekends,Global leaderboards with statistic,
First HoloSim, now UE5 shooter, 2 successful releases in a row + wonderful community week, Star Atlas is destined to be a hit with such a great team

author: [AEP] Lerinor [STAR]
content:
@Chri.z something occurred to me, regarding the idea of a limitless sink for components (tied to FICs sunset):

What if, instead of having infinity values for all upgrading components, you do it similar to current state? Let it go towards t5, and then reset the base again?

Reason: if all components are limitless, we all know for a fact that just a few components will be used, or even it would create a crazy run towards SDUs, maybe?

The current equilibrium in upgrading a base is working well (or at least we've adapted well to it).

content: I saw a guy mention token burns, and I was wondering ... will it work (be beneficial for the project in one form or another) that a percentage of the Atlas tokens used in Sage be burned? Part goes in DAO and ATMTA now, but what if something like 1% (or less) was permanently gone? We can always vote in the future to increase the token supply anyway, but it could maybe help the token price in the short term, and if the token price goes up, people often tend to jump in and take advantage of it, bringing a positive loop for the game and the economy.

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content: I have shared my thoughts on this a number of times. My personal viewpoint is that destroying value is not an optimal approach to creating value. Which is precisely what a token burn is - a destruction value. With the hope that the value destroyed gets recaptured by the remaining supply. Generally, I don't think that actually works, and has to "steal" value from some stakeholder. Now, it does make for a compelling narrative in crypto. People love it conceptually, whether or not it actually works fundamentally.   And just to flush this out with an example, let's take crafting fees in the game as an example, because that produces considerable value for the DAO each month. Crafting fees are set to 7% (rebalanced periodically) of the value of any item crafted in game. You could take 6% and transfer that to the DAO, and burn 1%. Now the DAO is making less revenue each month with the hope that a token burn narrative increases the exchange rate of the tokens. ATMTA could also burn some ATLAS income, but now we're making less money that we need to build the game. Or, we could take some revenue that would go to players and just burn it -- which I don't think any individual player would like.   So, the point is, if you want to burn tokens, someone has to lose potential value to achieve it.

author: BRUNN
content:
I’ve taken a close look at the concept of Zink and the zProfile, and I have a genuine question about the market problem this network is aiming to solve.
Many of the proposed features — single sign-on (SSO) for Web3, pre-configured permissions for dApps, on-chain reputation, Sybil resistance, and even integrated digital identity — are things that today can (and usually are) handled directly at the wallet level or through independent protocols like ENS, Lens, Gitcoin Passport, etc.

Bringing all of this to Layer 1 might offer native integration and standardization, but it also raises some concerns:

What’s the real advantage for the end user in replacing solutions already widely supported by multichain wallets, instead of using a network that “bakes” these features into the base layer?

Isn’t there a risk of closing the ecosystem and limiting user flexibility, when today they can choose the tools that best fit their workflow?

Adoption would require convincing developers to build exclusively on this Layer 1, which has historically been difficult when the value proposition could be delivered at the wallet or middleware level.

In short: the idea is interesting, but Zink seems to be taking on the role of a “super wallet” embedded in the blockchain. This raises the question: why create a new L1 for this instead of developing only a wallet with these features?

It would make much more sense to have a decentralized network optimized for gaming, which I’ve heard rumors about here. I don’t understand why this move is happening now.

author: Lysimach | Make Polis GreatMUD
content: As far as I know the fleet address is tied to the fleet name. E.g. if you disband a fleet and make a new one with the same name, it will also get the same address. That is also the reason why you can't give two fleets the same name. So there is no way to "just" rename a fleet, Sage/Holosim would always need to do these steps to "rename" a fleet: Unload all cargo, unload all crew, disband, create a new fleet with another name, load the crew, load the cargo. Yeah, unless they implement a completely new mechanism for the fleet name of course.

author: [SAG] Swift42 [CODE]
content: This feels more like an FR-chat discussion than an FR-room one, but since you raised this concern in a public channel, I think it’s fair to reply here.  No offense intended, brother, no worries! It’s perfectly natural for everyone to have their own point of view on this matter. Nothing is ever completely black or white — there’s a whole spectrum of greys in between XD  ATOM Cloud may have a “centralized nature” by definition, but it comes with zero maintenance cost, and you don’t sign anything in blood — the moment you disagree with the TOS, you can simply stop using it, just like any other tool.  On the other hand, a self-hosted solution doesn’t have an explicit hosting fee, but what about the electricity bill or the need to keep your internet connection always on? I did some math: assuming a 100W PC (which is less than an office PC), you spend about $10/month to keep it running 24/7. That’s perfectly fine for medium or bigger players, but it’s still a fixed cost that’s too high for smaller ones. I calculated using $0.15 per kWh — and let’s be real, most people use their gaming PC for this, and those machines consume way more than 100W.  Personally, I don’t like having a code injector on my PC either. I fully trust what the community builds, but I don’t trust TamperMonkey itself — and you never know how long it’ll be maintained.  So yeah, I agree with you: there are solutions for everyone. Personally, I really loved what ATMTA did with Anthony and Fleet Rentals — that’s a perfect example of how I’d leverage community tools that ATMTA considers valid!

content: Since you brought it up here: I controlled over 150 fleets with a Pi, which used only around 3W (=30 cents/month). Let's face it: SLYA will probably have problems to keep up with C4, because SLYA doesn't have a monetization model and Groove's and my free time is limited. Therefore I wish we have an official way to automate things in the cloud. Ok, this was my last message here, over and out

author: [DEEP] Digital Fists
content:
I strongly disagree with @Xcode's description (he already knows this) during the TH of Holosim automation being not perfect but ok. It is not ok for me. It is terrible.

I have around 12 fleets that should be constantly cycling through their actions, but instead, I get a few transactions a minute total, leaving nearly all of my fleets doing nothing, nearly all of the time. Meanwhile, everyone using Sly is racing ahead of me on the leaderboard. I have tried to play Holosim as it comes, and the experience has been poor.

I'm obviously not a new player, and I don't care about the prize pool, but new players trying to do well should not have such a huge disadvantage over people using 3rd party tools. And I thought the prizes were suppose to incentivise new players to join our community, so I don't see the point in giving the vast majority of the prizes away to existing community members who know how to exploit the game.

I'm sorry but Holosim seems like a bad advertisement of SA to me, and I've given up on competing in season 1 already. I hope season 2 or chapter 2 will be different.

author: [Λ] Neo_AArmstrong [EC]Econ Vantarah
content: @Chri.z Considering that MUDs and Usturs haven't yet closed the MRZ Starbases and that the main bottleneck of these bases is the SDU, and also considering that there's a high chance that LP will remain constantly high with ghost bases (since there are no resource limits on the bases, which can force LP above 28BI daily for all factions), how do you imagine the impact of this change on the value of SDUs and the slowdown caused in other starbases pending maximization? I ask this because at the current value, buying SDUs to install at 28BI (assuming this is the new normal if my projections are correct), the SDU would have an average -25% value upon installation and would only be minimally viable at 0.07 Atlas/unit. Installing SDUs with very high LPs would almost be throwing money away and very few players focused on capturing SDUs would actually be able to do this, but it is clearly one of the least profitable economic loops, greatly slowing down the progression of these bases.

author: Chri.z [STAR]
content:
To address several questions and concerns at once. We are well aware of the emissions issue and are closely monitoring the situation. It is my top priority to get ATLAS emissions back under control. However, we are still way under our initial planned emissions schedule. This alongside the desire to ensure players aren't going under water in SAGE given the price ratio of SOL/ATLAS, the temporary heightened emissions are OK in the short term. 

We want to address FICs but we can't meaningfully do anything with FICs with the LP loop in its current state. Hence, our launch of Phantom Starbases. So we had to solve the LP problem so players had a fallback when we started to curb back on FICs. We have a plan for FICs and I do want to start implementing it once we've established a healthy LP market. This will involve adjusting LP rewards for each of the starbase upgrading materials. 

I am aware that there is an SDU bottleneck. This bottleneck was difficult to solve because our game is on-chain. Once recipes have been started, you can't change them. I believe we can help alleviate SDU bottlenecks through various policies such as LP adjustments and supply adjustments. 

Our goal is to make this game affordable for small players again. that's much easier said than done in the current macro environment. But there are plenty of clever tricks we can use. In particular, the Ink launch provides a way for players to earn ATLAS with very little spending AND very little blockchain transaction costs. 

I read everything you guys send me and I appreciate your passion for SAGE. CC: @[DEEP] Digital Fists @ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics @[AEP] Mizz @[AEP] Lerinor @[HEIM] SDU pimp

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content:
I was thinking — I know this is probably the busiest moment of the last 4 years, and I’m not saying it should be done right now at all, but more as an idea for the future: many games, like CoD for example, have a mechanic where, through in-game tokens (not actual the in-game currency), you can activate double XP bonuses, usually split between weapon XP and profile XP.

It could be nice to implement something similar, adapting it to our own logic — so players could use these tokens to get 1 hour of double XP, or whatever timeframe we choose. The idea would be to craft these tokens in SAGE, leveraging the in-game economy and creating a resource burn (maybe even including INK itself).

Zink could serve as the bridge between the two: the activation of double XP would be tracked by the zProfile, which would then be read by the game client.

PS: You could not stack double exp weekend with those tokens maybe tokens need a different multiplier (lower) than 2x..

author: [-U-] OrionorbitMUD
content: there are scenarios where a component can buff one stat but debuff another. like an ammo module forcing a portion of the cargo hold to be dedicated to ammo. there are theoretical scenarios like that playing throughout the numbers but the numbers are still placeholder and the tool is designed to make it easy to edit and test any component or config at will to find the right balancing. some of the configs also will be trade offs for hit points, shield regen and damage, not all are just strict flat buffs for everything and is why there are so many types of configs.   can you show me which ships say no components loaded at t1/2? if you change to the config and the numbers in the config column change then those are being driven by loaded components.

author: ROME | Earther [ROME]
content: I've mentioned a few times that we will rotate some resources onto the ATLAS Locker Addendum and in-game regional governance (which requires DAO v2) after we ship C4. I think C4 will have a long lifespace, possibly indefinite. Probably a few more features, and continuous balance, but C4 is essentially the complete 4X gameplay loop. But I wouldn't anticipate any major overhauls after it's out. Never say never, but it will be a really solid foundation for years to come.
